data.title: AstraZeneca launches online platform offering meds at up to 70% off, benefiting patients with chronic conditions.
data.description: AstraZeneca Plc (NASDAQ: AZN) has introduced AstraZeneca Direct, an online platform aimed at providing a convenient way for qualifying patients to obtain their prescribed medications at a clear cash price, including home delivery. This service targets individuals dealing with chronic health conditions like asthma, diabetes, heart failure, and chronic kidney disease, as well as those wanting flu protection. As of October 1, 2025, eligible patients holding prescriptions for Airsupra (albuterol/budesonide) or Farxiga (dapaglifozin) can utilize AstraZeneca Direct to buy these medications directly.

data.title: Goldman raises S&P 500 target to 7,200 from 6,800, predicts two rate cuts by 2025, citing strong earnings growth.
data.description: Following a surge in Wall Street indices and the Federal Reserve indicating a dovish stance with potential rate cuts in the future, Goldman Sachs has revised its S&P 500 target. The bank's equity analyst, David Kostin, now forecasts a 12-month target of 7,200, up from the previous 6,800, attributing this bullish outlook to robust earnings, low positioning, and favorable interest rate conditions. The S&P 500 has already risen by 14% this year, primarily driven by earnings growth. Goldman anticipates two 25-basis point rate cuts by the end of 2025, with an additional two cuts in 2026, aligning with current market expectations. The focus remains on earnings as the principal factor propelling equity markets. For further details, refer to the full story on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Ex-U.S. Trade Rep Froman suggests smaller trade deals may replace multilateral system amid concerns.
data.description: According to Michael Froman, the former U.S. Trade Representative, trade agreements involving smaller clusters of nations are likely to supersede the struggling multilateral system.

data.title: BLS and other agencies face challenges reaching people for surveys and data collection as response rates decline.
data.description: Government statistical agencies, including the Bureau of Labor Statistics, and independent pollsters are facing upcoming challenges.

data.title: The USD's global value is declining rapidly, with a 7% decrease in 2025. It still leads in key financial sectors.
data.description: The U.S. dollar's status as the world's reserve currency is weakening, as per Goldman Sachs' report. Factors like shifting trade dynamics, weaker U.S. economic performance, and political risks are leading to a gradual depreciation of the dollar, with the trade-weighted dollar index already down 7% in 2025. Analysts predict further weakening due to decreased economic and market performance not justifying its high value. Despite this, the dollar still dominates in key areas, commanding over 60% of FX trading volumes, 50% of SWIFT transactions, 45% of cross-border debt issuance, and with a 40-50% share of international loans. However, signs of stress are evident, especially in the composition of central bank reserves where the dollar's share has been diminishing since 2015.

data.title: Fed's core PCE price index steady in August, supports rate cut outlook.
data.description: The core PCE price index, which is the Federal Reserve's key measure of inflation, remained stable in August. This data aligns with expectations and supports the outlook for a potential interest rate cut by the Fed.

data.title: Dogecoin down 17% in a week, but hopeful for rally with whale accumulation. Must hold $0.22 for potential $0.29 rebound.
data.description: Dogecoin (DOGE) has dropped by 17% in the last week, prompting concerns, but the accumulation by whales has sparked hope for a recovery. Crypto chart analyst Ali Martinez underlines the significance of Dogecoin maintaining a price above $0.22 for a potential rally towards $0.29. The emphasis is on Dogecoin needing to stay at $0.22 to potentially bounce back to $0.29, per Trader Daan Crypto Trades. For more details, access the complete article on Benzinga's website.

data.title: Nu Ride Inc. appoints Alexander Matina as CEO amid leadership transition. Shares traded as NRDE.
data.description: In a recent announcement on September 26, 2025, Nu Ride Inc. (OTCMKTS: NRDE), previously recognized as Lordstown Motors Corp., disclosed the appointment of Alexander Matina as the new Chief Executive Officer. Matina, a current member of the Board of Directors, will now lead the company in its operations. This change reflects a strategic decision by the company's Board of Directors to appoint Matina to the crucial role. Nu Ride Inc., a company operating in the automotive industry, is making significant moves with this leadership change.

data.title: CCS Facility Services partners with Benedictine College in higher education, expanding services.
data.description: A recent development in Denver highlights Benedictine College's expansion into the higher education sector through CCS Facility Services. This move signifies the growing influence of the college in the K12 sector. This advancement underscores Benedictine College's commitment to providing quality education while also diversifying its presence in the market. The partnership with CCS Facility Services indicates a strategic approach to enhancing educational offerings and reaching a broader audience. This development reflects the college's pursuit of innovation and growth, positioning it as a key player in the education industry.

data.title: 'Biometric Exit' program expands in U.S. airports, causing unease as federal officers take photos of departing intl passengers.
data.description: The initiative involves federal officers capturing photos of international travelers before their departure, and is expected to expand, which has sparked concerns about privacy.

data.title: Exhibition on 1937 Nanjing Massacre opens in Athens, marking first time Chinese memorial brings archives to Greece.
data.description: In Athens, Greece, on September 26, 2025, an exhibition on the 1937 Nanjing Massacre was inaugurated in Hall 7 of Zappeion Hall. This marks the inaugural presentation of the Chinese memorial commemorating the tragedy in Greece. The display will be available for public viewing for a certain period.

data.title: Pilot offering free any-size coffee at participating locations to celebrate National Coffee Day on September 29, 2025.
data.description: In Knoxville, Tennessee, Pilot, the leading network of travel centers known for the Best Coffee on the Interstate®, is enhancing autumn trips with seasonal coffee varieties and a fantastic deal for National Coffee Day. On September 29, 2025, patrons can visit any of Pilot's eligible outlets and relish a complimentary any-size hot coffee. Sean Marrero, the senior vice president of Pilot, expressed, "This National Coffee Day, we're delighting our customers with a wonderful cup of Pilot coffee." Don't miss this opportunity to grab a free coffee on National Coffee Day at Pilot locations across the country.

data.title: CarMax Q2 results disappoint, EPS at 64 cents vs $1.09 est. Sales at $6.594B (down 6% YoY) vs $7.024B est. 293K cars bought, down 2.4%. President remains confident in long-term strategy.
data.description: CarMax Inc (NYSE: KMX) reported disappointing second-quarter results with earnings per share at 64 cents, falling short of the expected $1.09. Quarterly sales totaled $6.594 billion, showing a 6% decline from the previous year and missing the anticipated $7.024 billion. The company purchased a total of 293,000 vehicles during the quarter, marking a 2.4% decrease. Out of these, 262,000 were acquired from consumers, dropping by 2.7%, while 31,000 were from dealers, increasing by 0.2%. Despite the challenging quarter, Bill Nash, the president, expressed confidence in the company's long-term strategy and the robust earnings model.
